response.justification: While the candidate demonstrates strong software engineering practices, broad technical leadership experience, and relevant infrastructure knowledge, they fail to meet critical minimum qualifications. Most significantly, only ~6 months of documented C++ experience versus the required 8 years is a disqualifying gap. Additionally, there is no evidence of production-level cloud compute platform architecture experience or a formal technical leadership role focused on cloud/DevOps domains.
strengths: 15+ years of diverse software development with demonstrated leadership across multiple senior/staff roles and proven ability to mentor teams through workshops and training programs
Hands-on experience designing and scaling large-scale distributed systems (micro-services handling millions of requests at Dunnhumby) with infrastructure-as-code skills (AWS CDK, Kubernetes, Docker)
Establishes engineering best practices and architectural patterns, with evidence of driving both internal improvements and open-source projects with rigorous design principles
concerns: Only ~6 months of documented C++ experience versus the required 8 years—a critical minimum qualification gap that disqualifies the candidate for this role

No evidence of production-level cloud compute platform architecture or strategy work; infrastructure experience is scattered across web, GIS, and data integration domains rather than focused on cloud compute
No formal technical leadership title or explicit record of mentoring cloud/DevOps engineers in a cloud infrastructure context; leadership experience is generalist rather than cloud-specialized
